Iron saturation [Mass Fraction] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated NT-proBNP, suggesting possible cardiac dysfunction or heart failure. There is dyslipidemia with high LDL and low HDL cholesterol, increasing cardiovascular risk. Erythrocyte count is slightly elevated but other hematological parameters are within normal limits. Renal, liver, and metabolic panels are unremarkable. Recommend clinical correlation for cardiac status and lipid management.

text.div: Provider Organization: St Matthews Health & Community Centre
